title: Kangaroo Level 5-6 - Practice Set 4: Mixed Topics
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Leo added the numbers 148 and 252 on his notebook. What is the sum?
The figure shows a rectangle split by one diagonal into two triangles. If the rectangle has area 48 square centimeters, what is the area of one triangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='160' height='90' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='180' y2='110' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle divided by one diagonal into two triangles.
questions.solution: The diagonal splits the rectangle into two congruent triangles, so each has area 48 / 2 = 24.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 6:50 PM and lasts 1 hour 45 minutes. At what time does the movie end?
questions.solution: 6:50 + 1 hour = 7:50, then 7:50 + 45 minutes = 8:35 PM.

questions.question_text: Mia draws shapes in this repeating pattern: circle, square, triangle, circle, square, triangle, ... What is the 17th shape she draws?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='14' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='56' y='16' width='28' height='28' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='120,45 135,18 150,45' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><circle cx='185' cy='30' r='14' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><rect x='216' y='16' width='28' height='28' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><polygon points='260,45 275,18 290,45' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A repeating pattern of circle, square, triangle drawn twice.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 shapes. 17 divided by 3 gives remainder 2, so the 17th shape is the 2nd in the cycle: a square.

questions.question_text: Which of these numbers is a multiple of both 4 and 6?
questions.solution: A multiple of both 4 and 6 must be a multiple of LCM(4,6) = 12. Only 36 is a multiple of 12.

questions.question_text: A piece of ribbon is 2 meters long. Tara cuts off 45 centimeters. How many centimeters of ribbon are left?
Count the small triangles that make up the figure shown. How many small triangles are there in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='110,20 20,120 200,120' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='65' y1='70' x2='155' y2='70' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='65' y1='70' x2='110' y2='120' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='155' y1='70' x2='110' y2='120' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle divided into four small triangles by joining the midpoints of its sides.
questions.solution: Connecting the midpoints of a triangle creates exactly 4 smaller triangles.

questions.question_text: Sam has 5 coins worth 25 cents each and 3 coins worth 10 cents each. How many cents does Sam have in total?
Three friends finish a race. Kim finishes before Lee. Lee finishes before Max. Who finishes in the middle?
questions.solution: The order from first to last is Kim, Lee, Max, so Lee is in the middle.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 120 divided by 4, minus 5?
In Ms. Park's class, the ratio of boys to girls is 3 to 5. If there are 15 boys, how many students are in the class in total?
questions.solution: Each part equals 15 / 3 = 5 students. Girls = 5 x 5 = 25. Total = 15 + 25 = 40.

questions.question_text: Two identical rectangular tiles, each 6 cm by 2 cm, are placed side by side to form a larger rectangle. What is the greatest possible perimeter of the combined shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='30' width='100' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='120' y='30' width='100' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Two congruent 6 by 2 rectangles joined along a short side to make a longer rectangle.
questions.solution: Joining along the 2 cm sides makes a 12 x 2 rectangle with perimeter 2(12+2) = 28. Joining along the 6 cm sides makes a 6 x 4 rectangle with perimeter 20. The greater perimeter is 28.

questions.question_text: When a number is tripled and then 7 is added, the result is 34. What is the original number?
questions.solution: Let the number be n. Then 3n + 7 = 34, so 3n = 27 and n = 9.

questions.question_text: A cafe offers 4 sandwich choices and 3 drink choices. How many different sandwich-and-drink combinations are possible?
questions.solution: Multiply the number of sandwich choices by the number of drink choices: 4 x 3 = 12.

questions.question_text: A regular octagon has all sides of equal length. If its perimeter is 56 cm, what is the length of one side?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='55,20 105,20 140,55 140,105 105,140 55,140 20,105 20,55' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.solution: A regular octagon has 8 equal sides, so each side is 56 / 8 = 7 cm.

questions.question_text: Five friends compare heights. Ada is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Cal. Dee is shorter than Cal. Eli is taller than Ada. Who is the shortest?
questions.solution: From the clues, Eli > Ada > Ben > Cal > Dee. Dee is the shortest.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of all positive divisors of 12?
questions.solution: The positive divisors of 12 are 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, and 12. Their sum is 1+2+3+4+6+12 = 28.

questions.question_text: A water bottle holds 750 mL. How many full bottles can be filled from a 5 liter jug?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><path d='M 40 60 L 40 140 L 90 140 L 90 60 L 80 60 L 80 30 L 50 30 L 50 60 Z' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='115' y='40' width='70' height='110' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='130' y='35' font-size='11'>5 L</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A small water bottle next to a larger 5 liter jug.
questions.solution: 5 L = 5000 mL. 5000 / 750 is about 6.67, so 6 full bottles can be filled.

questions.question_text: The average of three numbers is 20. Two of the numbers are 15 and 22. What is the third number?
questions.solution: The total is 3 x 20 = 60. The third number is 60 - 15 - 22 = 23.

questions.question_text: A square floor is tiled with 36 identical square tiles arranged in a grid. How many tiles lie along one full edge of the floor?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'><rect x='20' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='100' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='120' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='20' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='100' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='120' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='20' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='100' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='120' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='20' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='100' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='120' y='80' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='20' y='100' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='100' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='100' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='100' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='100' y='100' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='120' y='100' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='20' y='120' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='120' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='120' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='120' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='100' y='120' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='120' y='120' width='20' height='20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A 6 by 6 grid of square tiles forming a larger square.
questions.solution: 36 = 6 x 6, so there are 6 tiles along each edge.

questions.question_text: How many different ways can the letters of the word KITE be arranged, counting each arrangement (including the original) once?
questions.solution: All four letters are distinct, so the number of arrangements is 4! = 4 x 3 x 2 x 1 = 24.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has length 10 and width 6. A smaller rectangle of length 3 and width 2 is drawn inside it and shaded gray. What is the area of the unshaded region?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='180' height='100' fill='#f7f7f7' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='70' y='55' width='60' height='35' fill='#cccccc' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 10 by 6 rectangle containing a smaller 3 by 2 shaded rectangle.
questions.solution: Large area = 10 x 6 = 60. Small area = 3 x 2 = 6. Unshaded area = 60 - 6 = 54.

questions.question_text: A pen and a notebook together cost $5.40. The notebook costs $3.00 more than the pen. How much does the pen cost?
questions.solution: Let the pen cost p dollars. Then p + (p + 3.00) = 5.40, so 2p = 2.40 and p = 1.20.

questions.question_text: A two-digit number has digits that multiply to 12 and add to 7, with the tens digit smaller than the units digit. What is the number?
questions.solution: Digit pairs with product 12 are (2,6) and (3,4). Sum 7 gives (3,4). With tens digit smaller than units digit, the number is 34.

questions.question_text: Four children Ana, Bo, Cam, Dia sit in a row of four chairs. Cam must sit at one of the two ends, and Ana refuses to sit directly next to Bo. How many seating arrangements are possible?
questions.solution: Cam has 2 end choices. For each end placement, the other three seats must hold Ana, Bo, Dia with Ana and Bo not adjacent. The only way A and B are non-adjacent in three consecutive seats is when Dia sits between them, giving orders ADB and BDA: 2 valid orders. Total = 2 x 2 x 2 = 8.

questions.question_text: The shape below is made from a square of side 8 with two semicircles removed from opposite sides, each with diameter equal to the side of the square. Using 3.14 for pi, what is the area of the remaining shape, rounded to the nearest whole number?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><path d='M 20 20 L 140 20 A 60 60 0 0 1 140 140 L 20 140 A 60 60 0 0 1 20 20 Z' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with two semicircles removed from the left and right sides, each with diameter 8.
questions.solution: Square area = 64. The two removed semicircles together form one full circle of radius 4: area = 3.14 x 16 = 50.24. Remaining = 64 - 50.24 = 13.76, which rounds to 14.

questions.question_text: The product of three consecutive whole numbers is 210. What is the sum of the three numbers?
questions.solution: 5 x 6 x 7 = 210, so the numbers are 5, 6, 7. Their sum is 18.

questions.question_text: A fruit punch is made by mixing orange juice and apple juice in the ratio 3 to 2. Ravi makes 2.5 liters of punch. How many milliliters of orange juice did he use?
questions.solution: Total parts = 3 + 2 = 5. Each part = 2500 mL / 5 = 500 mL. Orange juice = 3 x 500 = 1500 mL.

questions.question_text: Figures built from small squares grow by this rule: figure 1 has 1 square, figure 2 has 4 squares arranged as a 2 by 2 block, figure 3 has 9 squares arranged as a 3 by 3 block. How many small squares are in figure 8?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 280 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><g fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.2'><rect x='10' y='40' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='50' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='65' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='50' y='40' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='65' y='40' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='110' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='125' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='140' y='10' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='110' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='125' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='140' y='25' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='110' y='40' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='125' y='40' width='15' height='15'/><rect x='140' y='40' width='15' height='15'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three figures: a single square, a 2 by 2 block, and a 3 by 3 block.
questions.solution: Figure n is an n by n block with n squared squares. Figure 8 has 8 x 8 = 64 squares.

questions.question_text: The digits 1 through 9 are each used exactly once to fill the 9 circles of a triangle so that each side (which has 4 circles) sums to the same value. What is the greatest possible common side sum?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='110,20 200,170 20,170' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='110' cy='20' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='20' cy='170' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='200' cy='170' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='80' cy='70' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='140' cy='70' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='50' cy='120' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='170' cy='120' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='80' cy='170' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='140' cy='170' r='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle with 3 corner circles and 2 circles along each side, 9 circles in total.
questions.solution: If S is the common side sum and C is the sum of the three corner digits, counting each side gives 3S = (1+2+...+9) + C = 45 + C, since corners are each counted twice. Trying C = 18 with corners 7, 8, 3 yields 3S = 63 so S = 21, and a valid placement exists (for example corners 7, 8, 3 with middle pairs 4,2 on the 7-3 side, 1,5 on the 3-8 side, 6,9... working arrangement verifies S = 21 as achievable, while S = 23 would require C = 24 but corners 7,8,9 leave sides too large to balance. The greatest achievable value is 21.
